scenarios.query:
Review this adoption proposal.
```text
PR: "Add Kafka for the notifications feature"
Rationale: "We need a queue." Adds three services (Kafka, Zookeeper, schema-registry) to docker-compose.
No ADR. Notifications volume: ~200 messages/day. No mention of alternatives, or of how we would remove it later.
```
expected_behavior: Flags the missing adoption justification — "we need a queue" is not a rationale; expects alternatives weighed (e.g. a DB-backed queue or cron job for ~200 messages/day)
Flags right-sizing: Kafka + Zookeeper + schema-registry is far too heavy for 200 messages/day (cross-links restraint #11)
Flags the missing decision record and the absence of any lock-in / exit consideration
Recommends recording the decision (context, options, consequences) or choosing a lighter option — does not just approve

scenarios.query:
Review whether this accepted decision still holds.
```text
ADR-12 (accepted 2023): "Use a single Postgres instance; team is 3 engineers, < 50 req/s, no HA requirement."
Today: team is 25 engineers, 1,200 req/s at peak, a 4-hour outage last quarter (incident #88), still no replica.
No revisit-trigger was ever recorded.
```
expected_behavior: Recognizes the decision's stated assumptions (3 engineers, < 50 req/s, no HA) have expired
Flags that the decision is now stale debt and should be revisited / superseded (HA, a replica)
Notes the absence of a revisit-trigger as why it rotted silently, and recommends adding one
Judges it against changed conditions — does not call the original 2023 decision wrong at the time it was made

scenarios.query:
Review this deprecation plan.
```text
RFC: deprecate the v1 /reports API.
- v1 marked `deprecated: true` in the OpenAPI spec; Sunset header set to 2026-12-01 (RFC 8594)
- migration guide to v2 published; the top 5 consumers have been contacted
- removal ticket PLAT-441 scheduled for after the sunset date; changelog "Deprecated" entry added
```
expected_behavior: Reports no substantive findings — retirement is planned and clocked (sunset date, migration path, tracked removal ticket, changelog entry)
Confirms the Sunset header / deprecation marker and the consumer-migration path are present
Does not invent problems or demand process beyond what a well-signaled deprecation needs

scenarios.query:
Review this build-vs-buy decision.
```text
ADR-30: "Build our own billing & invoicing engine instead of adopting Stripe Billing."
Rationale: "More control." No TCO comparison, no estimate of maintenance cost, no exit/migration consideration. Pricing and contract terms not discussed.
```
expected_behavior: Flags the thin rationale ("more control") with no build-vs-buy comparison or maintenance/TCO estimate
Flags the missing exit / lock-in consideration on a one-way-door choice (a billing engine is expensive to reverse)
Escalates the TCO / procurement / contract-terms judgment to humans rather than adjudicating the business call (the G8 detect-and-escalate boundary)
Recommends recording the options considered and the assumptions in the ADR
